By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Shrine|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:田嶋神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Shrine|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:田嶋神社]]

'''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:田嶋神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tajima Shrine (Imari)-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:田嶋神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]][[ja:田嶋神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: - '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. - '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == - '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan - '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. - '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishimahime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho,
