Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Hōya)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishimahime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Hōya|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishimahime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishimahime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tajima Shrine (Imari)}}

Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishimahime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=draft}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=draft}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=draft}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=draft}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|12=draft}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|12=draft}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tajima Shrine (Imari)}}

Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tajima Shrine (Imari)}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishimahime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=draft|12=draft}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=draft|12=draft}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tajima Shrine (Imari)}}

revision.comment: 41 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:田嶋神社 up to rev 100464926
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|佐賀県伊万里市の田嶋神社|佐賀県唐津市の同名の神社|田島神社}} {{神社|名称=田嶋神社|画像=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
田嶋神社の社殿（覆屋）|所在地=伊万里市波多津町畑津1260|祭神=宗像三女神(多岐津姫命, 市杵島姫命, 田心姫命） |ISO=JP-41 |地図=Japan Saga | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 =21 | 緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 =129 | 経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 30 }} '''田嶋神社'''（たしまじんじゃ）とは[[佐賀県]][[伊万里市]]波多津町にある神社である。 == 概要 == 現存する神社の本殿としては九州内最古とされ、15世紀建立と推定される。 中世領主である[[波多氏]]の津(港)であったことから「波多津」と呼ばれたこの地域の守り神として、[[佐賀県]][[唐津市]][[呼子町]][[加部島]]の[[田島神社]]から分霊を勧請したと考えられている。 本殿は、簡素な造りだがかつては朱塗りの彩色社殿であったと考えられており、現在は本殿を保護する為の[[覆堂|覆屋]](おおいや)が設けられており、覆屋の格子越しに見学可能である。貴重な建築物として国重要文化財に指定されている。 == 祭神 == 宗像三女神（[[タキリビメ|田心姫神]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫神]]）を主神とし、八柱をまつっている。{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.imari.saga.jp/2973.htm |title=田嶋神社本殿/伊万里市 |access-date=2023-03-12 |publisher=伊万里市}} == 社殿 == '''田嶋神社'''は佐賀県伊万里市波多津町畑津1260番地にある。明治6年（1873）年旧村社となった。建武元（1334）年に再建したと伝えられている。{{Cite book|和書|title=佐賀県神社誌|date=1926-11-15|publisher=佐賀県神職会}} 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多岐津姫命]]・[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]・[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]の[[宗像三女神]]を主神とし、明治時代に周辺の[[無格社]]が祀っていた菅原道真・伊弉諾尊・伊弉冉巫女・大国主命・天忍穂耳命を合わせて八柱を祀っている。 '''本殿'''は三間社流見世棚造という建築様式で造られ、切妻桟瓦葺覆屋の中にある。 '''身舎'''は床下が八角形、床上内側は正面中柱を除いて全て十六角形になっている。身舎部の柱は自然石の礎石上にたち、地貫・腰長押・内法長押・頭貫で固められている。柱頭に平三斗を置き、四隅は連斗なしの連三斗である。四隅の秤肘木は、正面と背面側を秤肘木型につくらず、木鼻をほぼ垂直に切断（鯖尾形）している。中備はまったくなく、妻飾りは豕扠首である。正面の三間は幣軸付き板唐戸を立てている。内部は床が板張りで、天井は竿縁天井、背面に高棚を設けている。 '''見世棚'''は大面取り角柱を自然石の礎石上にたて、下端面取りの頭貫を渡している。ただし中央間は茨垂木状で、やはり下部に面を取っている。柱頭は出三斗で、両端は頭貫木鼻を根肘木がわりにして連三斗とする。肘木はすべて下端に面を取っている。身舎とは繋虹梁で結び、中備は中央間のみ斗を配している。見世棚（向拝部分）の桁や垂木から上は、後世の新しい補修である。正面中央間に木階が付いていたと考えられる。 元は朱塗りの彩色社殿であり、一部に残存色がある。近世の神社建築と比較して、全体に簡潔な造りの素晴らしい社殿である。再建年代は建武元（1334）年と伝わっているが、それを否定する点は現在のところ見いだせない。{{Cite book|和書|title=伊万里市の文化財─ふるさとの風土と歴史を知る宝物─|date=2002-03-31|publisher=伊万里市教育委員会}}{{Cite book|和書|title=伊万里市史・建築編 |date=2002-03|publisher=伊万里市史編纂委員会・伊万里市}}長享2年（1488）建立の大分県日田市前津江町所在の、大野老松天満宮社旧本殿（国重要文化財・建造物）との類似性があると考えられている。これらのことから少なくとも15世紀前半（室町時代前期・1392～1467）の建造物であると考えられ、現存する社殿（本殿）建造物としては九州最古の貴重な遺構である。{{Cite book|和書|title=重要文化財　大野老松天満宮社旧本殿　修理工事報告書|year=1984|publisher=大野老松天満社旧本殿修理委員会}}　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　 ※本殿は覆屋の中にあり、原則として覆屋の格子越しにしか見学できない。 [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 02.jpg|サムネイル|本殿向拝部分]] [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 09.jpg|サムネイル|連三斗（つれみつど）]] [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 03.jpg|サムネイル|鯖尾形の木鼻]] [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 01.jpg|サムネイル|杮（こけら）葺きの屋根]] == 歴史 == 唐津市呼子町加部島にある[[田島神社]]の末社とされる。創建年代は、加部島の田島神社が天平年代（730年頃）ではないかとされるが、波多津の田嶋神社は、神社の記録によると建武元年(1336年)に社殿が再建され、室町時代末期には岸岳城主波多氏の祈願所だったと伝えられている。 荒波激しい玄界灘に浮かぶ加部島の田島神社は古来「末盧(まつろ)国」の海路の安全と海産業の豊漁を願った「田島坐神社(たじまにゐますかみのやしろ)」として祀られていたようである。ここを基に、旧唐津領内に「田島神社」の流れを汲んだ社は数多く存在する。 == 神事・祭事{{Cite book|和書 |title=波多津町誌|date=1999-03-30|publisher=伊万里市波多津町誌編集委員会}} == *1月1日 - 正月祭り　宮惣代参列・宮司により司る祭 *3月17日 - 記念祭　宮惣代参列・宮司により司る祭 *7月14日 - 祇園祭　前夜祭　宮司・宮惣代・区長参拝 *7月15日 - 本祭り　宮惣代・区長参列　馬蛤潟にお下り *10月16日 - 田島祭　宮惣代・区長参拝 *10月17日 - 宮惣代　浦新地へお下り　浦区民は皆参拝する供日 *12月30日 - 大抜祭　年越行事 == 文化財 == === 国指定重要文化財　建造物 === * 田嶋神社本殿（昭和62年6月3日指定）[[ファイル:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|サムネイル|重要文化財指定記念碑]] [[ファイル:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|サムネイル|国指定重要文化財指定書]] == 交通アクセス == 〒848-0102　[[佐賀県]][[伊万里市]][[波多津町]]畑津1260番地 JR[[筑肥線]]・[[松浦鉄道]]西九州線[[伊万里駅]]より車で約35分 最寄バス停　[[西肥バス]] 内野口 == 出典 == {{DEFAULTSORT:たしましんしや}} [[Category:佐賀県の神社]] [[Category:伊万里市の歴史]] [[Category:佐賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:伊万里市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[田嶋神社]] to [[Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|佐賀県伊万里市の田嶋神社|佐賀県唐津市の同名の神社|田島神社}} {{神社|名称=田嶋神社|画像=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
田嶋神社の社殿（覆屋）|所在地=伊万里市波多津町畑津1260|祭神=宗像三女神(多岐津姫命, 市杵島姫命, 田心姫命） |ISO=JP-41 |地図=Japan Saga | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 =21 | 緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 =129 | 経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 30 }} '''田嶋神社'''（たしまじんじゃ）とは[[佐賀県]][[伊万里市]]波多津町にある神社である。 == 概要 == 現存する神社の本殿としては九州内最古とされ、15世紀建立と推定される。 中世領主である[[波多氏]]の津(港)であったことから「波多津」と呼ばれたこの地域の守り神として、[[佐賀県]][[唐津市]][[呼子町]][[加部島]]の[[田島神社]]から分霊を勧請したと考えられている。 本殿は、簡素な造りだがかつては朱塗りの彩色社殿であったと考えられており、現在は本殿を保護する為の[[覆堂|覆屋]](おおいや)が設けられており、覆屋の格子越しに見学可能である。貴重な建築物として国重要文化財に指定されている。 == 祭神 == 宗像三女神（[[タキリビメ|田心姫神]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫神]]）を主神とし、八柱をまつっている。{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.imari.saga.jp/2973.htm |title=田嶋神社本殿/伊万里市 |access-date=2023-03-12 |publisher=伊万里市}} == 社殿 == '''田嶋神社'''は佐賀県伊万里市波多津町畑津1260番地にある。明治6年（1873）年旧村社となった。建武元（1334）年に再建したと伝えられている。{{Cite book|和書|title=佐賀県神社誌|date=1926-11-15|publisher=佐賀県神職会}} 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多岐津姫命]]・[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]・[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]の[[宗像三女神]]を主神とし、明治時代に周辺の[[無格社]]が祀っていた菅原道真・伊弉諾尊・伊弉冉巫女・大国主命・天忍穂耳命を合わせて八柱を祀っている。 '''本殿'''は三間社流見世棚造という建築様式で造られ、切妻桟瓦葺覆屋の中にある。 '''身舎'''は床下が八角形、床上内側は正面中柱を除いて全て十六角形になっている。身舎部の柱は自然石の礎石上にたち、地貫・腰長押・内法長押・頭貫で固められている。柱頭に平三斗を置き、四隅は連斗なしの連三斗である。四隅の秤肘木は、正面と背面側を秤肘木型につくらず、木鼻をほぼ垂直に切断（鯖尾形）している。中備はまったくなく、妻飾りは豕扠首である。正面の三間は幣軸付き板唐戸を立てている。内部は床が板張りで、天井は竿縁天井、背面に高棚を設けている。 '''見世棚'''は大面取り角柱を自然石の礎石上にたて、下端面取りの頭貫を渡している。ただし中央間は茨垂木状で、やはり下部に面を取っている。柱頭は出三斗で、両端は頭貫木鼻を根肘木がわりにして連三斗とする。肘木はすべて下端に面を取っている。身舎とは繋虹梁で結び、中備は中央間のみ斗を配している。見世棚（向拝部分）の桁や垂木から上は、後世の新しい補修である。正面中央間に木階が付いていたと考えられる。 元は朱塗りの彩色社殿であり、一部に残存色がある。近世の神社建築と比較して、全体に簡潔な造りの素晴らしい社殿である。再建年代は建武元（1334）年と伝わっているが、それを否定する点は現在のところ見いだせない。{{Cite book|和書|title=伊万里市の文化財─ふるさとの風土と歴史を知る宝物─|date=2002-03-31|publisher=伊万里市教育委員会}}{{Cite book|和書|title=伊万里市史・建築編 |date=2002-03|publisher=伊万里市史編纂委員会・伊万里市}}長享2年（1488）建立の大分県日田市前津江町所在の、大野老松天満宮社旧本殿（国重要文化財・建造物）との類似性があると考えられている。これらのことから少なくとも15世紀前半（室町時代前期・1392～1467）の建造物であると考えられ、現存する社殿（本殿）建造物としては九州最古の貴重な遺構である。{{Cite book|和書|title=重要文化財　大野老松天満宮社旧本殿　修理工事報告書|year=1984|publisher=大野老松天満社旧本殿修理委員会}}　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　 ※本殿は覆屋の中にあり、原則として覆屋の格子越しにしか見学できない。 [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 02.jpg|サムネイル|本殿向拝部分]] [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 09.jpg|サムネイル|連三斗（つれみつど）]] [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 03.jpg|サムネイル|鯖尾形の木鼻]] [[ファイル:田嶋神社本殿 01.jpg|サムネイル|杮（こけら）葺きの屋根]] == 歴史 == 唐津市呼子町加部島にある[[田島神社]]の末社とされる。創建年代は、加部島の田島神社が天平年代（730年頃）ではないかとされるが、波多津の田嶋神社は、神社の記録によると建武元年(1336年)に社殿が再建され、室町時代末期には岸岳城主波多氏の祈願所だったと伝えられている。 荒波激しい玄界灘に浮かぶ加部島の田島神社は古来「末盧(まつろ)国」の海路の安全と海産業の豊漁を願った「田島坐神社(たじまにゐますかみのやしろ)」として祀られていたようである。ここを基に、旧唐津領内に「田島神社」の流れを汲んだ社は数多く存在する。 == 神事・祭事{{Cite book|和書 |title=波多津町誌|date=1999-03-30|publisher=伊万里市波多津町誌編集委員会}} == *1月1日 - 正月祭り　宮惣代参列・宮司により司る祭 *3月17日 - 記念祭　宮惣代参列・宮司により司る祭 *7月14日 - 祇園祭　前夜祭　宮司・宮惣代・区長参拝 *7月15日 - 本祭り　宮惣代・区長参列　馬蛤潟にお下り *10月16日 - 田島祭　宮惣代・区長参拝 *10月17日 - 宮惣代　浦新地へお下り　浦区民は皆参拝する供日 *12月30日 - 大抜祭　年越行事 == 文化財 == === 国指定重要文化財　建造物 === * 田嶋神社本殿（昭和62年6月3日指定）[[ファイル:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|サムネイル|重要文化財指定記念碑]] [[ファイル:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|サムネイル|国指定重要文化財指定書]] == 交通アクセス == 〒848-0102　[[佐賀県]][[伊万里市]][[波多津町]]畑津1260番地 JR[[筑肥線]]・[[松浦鉄道]]西九州線[[伊万里駅]]より車で約35分 最寄バス停　[[西肥バス]] 内野口 == 出典 == {{DEFAULTSORT:たしましんしや}} [[Category:佐賀県の神社]] [[Category:伊万里市の歴史]] [[Category:佐賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:伊万里市の建築物]]

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishimahime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tajima Shrine (Imari)}} {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tajima Shrine (Imari)}} {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|田島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashima Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tajima Shrine (Imari)}} {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]]

By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] [[Category:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]]

Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|ja|伊万里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imari Station|qq=}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]]

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]]

As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[en:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[Category:伊万里市の建築物]] [[Category:伊万里市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:佐賀県の重要文化財]]

It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imari]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imari]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Imari, Saga]] [[Category:伊万里市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Saga prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tashima-jinja (Imari)}}

Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Imari, Saga]] [[Category:伊万里市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Saga prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tashima-jinja (Imari)}}

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Tashima Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|250px]]
Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Imari, Saga]] [[Category:伊万里の Municipal History]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Saga prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tashima-jinja (Imari)}}

Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Imari, Saga]] [[Category:Municipal History of Imari]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Saga prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tashima-jinja (Imari)}}
