Tashima Shrine Main Hall (Fukudō)|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity=Munakata Three Goddesses (Tagitsu-hime, Ichikishima-hime, Tagiri-hime)|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30}} '''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan. == Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century. As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City. The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities. == Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334). The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design. Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible. Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu. == History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan. Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage. == Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]] == Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line. == References == {{translated page|ja|田嶋神社|version=100464926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[sn:Tajima Shrine (Imari)]] [[ja:田嶋神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Imari, Saga]] [[Category:Municipal History of Imari]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Saga prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tashima-jinja (Imari)}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q117305592]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767}}|name=Tashima Shrine|image=File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity={{ill|Ichikishimahime|qid=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Tagitsuhime|qid=Q10932592}}; {{ill|Takiribime|qid=Q10932870}}|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30|prefecture={{ill|Imari|qid=Q857266}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.3651077|N|129.8751127|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.

'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]

== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tashima-jinja (Imari)]][[ja:田嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q117305592]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}}|name=Tashima Shrine|image=File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity={{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Tagitsuhime|WD=Q10932592}}; {{ill|Takiribime|WD=Q10932870}}|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30|prefecture={{ill|Imari|WD=Q857266}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.3651077|N|129.8751127|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.

'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]

'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]], Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of [[:en:Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)]] in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR [[:en:Imari Station|Imari Station]]. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imari, Saga|en|Imari, Saga|WD=Q857266|lt=Imari}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|en|Imari Station|WD=Q8189881|lt=Imari Station}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tashima-jinja (Imari)]][[ja:田嶋神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tashima-jinja (Imari)]][[ja:田嶋神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tashima-jinja (Imari)]][[ja:田嶋神社]]
{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}}|name=Tashima Shrine|image=File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity={{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Tagitsuhime|WD=Q10932592}}; {{ill|Takiribime|WD=Q10932870}}|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30|prefecture={{ill|Imari|WD=Q857266}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.3651077|N|129.8751127|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imari, Saga|en|Imari, Saga|WD=Q857266|lt=Imari}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|en|Imari Station|WD=Q8189881|lt=Imari Station}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.
== References == <references />

[[ja:田嶋神社]]

The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|1=Tashima Shrine (Saga)}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|en|Imari Station|WD=Q8189881|lt=Imari Station|1=Imari Station}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.

'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imari, Saga|en|Imari, Saga|WD=Q857266|lt=Imari|1=Imari, Saga}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|1=Tashima Shrine (Saga)}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|1=Tashima Shrine (Saga)}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.

{{Otheruses|Tashima Shrine in Imari, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine in Karatsu, Saga Prefecture|Tashima Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767|1=Shinto}}|name=Tashima Shrine|image=File:田嶋神社社殿.jpg|location=1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari-shi, Saga Prefecture|deity={{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454|1=Ichikishima-hime}}; {{ill|Tagitsuhime|WD=Q10932592|1=Tagitsuhime}}; {{ill|Takiribime|WD=Q10932870|1=Tagorihime}}|ISO=JP-41|Map=Japan Saga|LatDeg=33|LatMin=21|LatSec=54|LongDeg=129|LongMin=52|LongSec=30|prefecture={{ill|Imari|WD=Q857266|1=Imari, Saga}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.3651077|N|129.8751127|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Tashima Shrine''' (田嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imari, Saga|en|Imari, Saga|WD=Q857266|lt=Imari|1=Imari, Saga}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The main hall of Tashima Shrine is considered the oldest existing shrine building in Kyushu, estimated to have been built in the 15th century.
As the guardian deity of the area, historically called "Hatatsu" due to its connection with the Hatatsu clan's port (津, tsu), the shrine is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from the {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|1=Tashima Shrine (Saga)}} in {{ill|Kabe Island|ja|加部島|ceb|Kabe_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Saga-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabe Island|qq=}}, Karatsu City.
The main hall, though simple in design, is thought to have been originally painted in red. It is now enclosed in a protective {{ill|Fukudō|ja|覆堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukudō|qq=}} (protective structure) and can be viewed through lattice openings. This historic structure is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.
== deity == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Tagorihime|lt=Tagorihime|lt=Takiribime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as well as five additional deities.
== Shrine Structure == The shrine is located at 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture. It was designated as a village shrine in 1873. According to records, the main hall was rebuilt in Kenmu 1 (1334).
The main hall is constructed in the "Sangen-sha Ryumisedana-zukuri" style, enclosed in a hōya with a gabled roof. It features an octagonal lower structure and a sixteen-sided upper interior design.
Unique architectural details include: * '''Miya''': Elevated on natural stone foundation, with intricately joined beams and columns. * '''Misedana (Display Shelves)''': Constructed with prominent corner posts and a connecting rainbow beam. - Original vermilion-painted surfaces with traces of color still visible.
Comparisons have been drawn to the former main hall of {{ill|Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja|大野老松天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Oimatsu Tenmangū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Hita City, Oita Prefecture, built in 1488. This supports the assessment that Tashima Shrine dates back to the early 15th century, making it the oldest extant shrine building in Kyushu.
== History == Tashima Shrine is considered a branch of {{ill|Tashima Shrine|en|Tashima Shrine|WD=Q11576656|lt=Tashima Shrine (Karatsu)|1=Tashima Shrine (Saga)}} in Kabeshima, Karatsu City. While the main shrine in Kabeshima likely dates to the Tenpyō era (~730 AD), records indicate the Hatatsu shrine was rebuilt in 1336. By the late Muromachi period, it served as the prayer site for the Kishi-ga-take Castle lord of the Hatatsu clan.
Located on the stormy Genkai Sea, the Kabeshima shrine historically served as a protective deity for sea routes and fisheries. Numerous shrines bearing the name "Tashima" in the former Karatsu domain share this lineage.
== Rituals and Festivals == * '''January 1st''' - New Year's Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''March 17th''' - Commemorative Festival: Conducted by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''July 14th''' - Gion Festival Eve: Attended by shrine officials, local representatives, and community leaders. * '''July 15th''' - Main Gion Festival: Features a ritual procession to Makai-ga-shita. * '''October 16th''' - Tashima Festival: Rituals performed by shrine officials and local representatives. * '''October 17th''' - Community Festival: Procession to Ura-shinchi, attended by all local residents. * '''December 30th''' - Year-End Purification: A traditional New Year’s Eve ceremony.
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (Structure) === * Main Hall of Tashima Shrine (designated June 3, 1987) [[File:重要文化財指定記念碑.jpg|thumb|Memorial for Important Cultural Property Designation]] [[File:重要文化財指定書建第2191号のコピー.jpg|thumb|Certificate of Important Cultural Property Designation]]
== Access == * '''Address''': 1260 Hatatsu, Hatatsu-cho, Imari City, Saga Prefecture, Japan * '''By Car''': Approximately 35 minutes from JR {{ill|Imari Station|en|Imari Station|WD=Q8189881|lt=Imari Station|1=Imari Station}}. * '''Bus''': Nearest stop is Uchino-guchi on the Nishitetsu Bus line.
